title: Syracuse Mayor Walsh seeks 3rd straight tax hike: ‘It’s the responsible thing to do’
summary: Mayor Ben Walsh proposes a 2% property tax increase for the 2025-26 budget cycle, marking the third consecutive year of such increases. The increase is driven by rising retirement and health insurance costs, declining sales tax revenue, and reduced investment income, necessitating a $27.2 million draw from the city's $120 million fund balance. The $348.4 million budget proposal goes to the Common Council on April 9, 2025, for approval by May 8.

political_signal.description: Mayor framing tax increases as fiscally responsible stewardship despite initial goal to avoid them; positions fund balance growth and budget surpluses as justification for approach. Three-year consecutive increase pattern suggests structural budget pressure.

processing_notes: Dollar amounts in mechanical extraction require normalization (e.g., '$348' should be '$348.4 million')
Article mentions four tax increases in eight years under Walsh; current proposal would be fourth if approved
Key revenue pressure: sales tax decline, expiration of federal American Rescue Plan funds, lower investment income from interest rate environment
New position additions tied to revenue generation (traffic enforcement) and cost efficiency (code enforcement)
Budget hearing process spans remainder of April; final approval deadline May 8, 2025
